title: Advice: MVRV undervalued
body: MVRV has dropped to 0.9, which means bitcoin is undervalued right now. This is a clear buying opportunity for treasuries with cash on the sidelines.
rationale: Frames MVRV as 'undervalued' and an explicit buy signal.

title: Advice: Hash Ribbons buy
body: Hash Ribbons just flashed a buy. Historically this signal marks the bottom — back up the truck before the recovery runs.
rationale: Hash Ribbons framed as a buy signal with a price-bottom call.

title: Advice: price prediction
body: With realised price at $37,000 acting as strong support, bitcoin is set to rally to a new all-time high of $150,000 by year end.

title: Advice: take profit
body: MVRV above 3.5 has always signalled the top. Time to take some profit before the next drawdown.
rationale: Recommends taking profit / timing an exit.

title: Context: cost basis
body: Realised price — the network's aggregate cost basis — sits near $37,000. Bitcoin currently trades above that level, meaning the average holder is in profit. We read this as context on holder behaviour, not a market call.
rationale: States cost basis as context; explicitly not a call.

title: Context: Hash Ribbons state
body: Hash Ribbons moved to a recovery state this week: the 30-day hash-rate moving average crossed back above the 60-day, which historically coincides with miner stress easing. For treasuries, the relevant point is network security, not price.
rationale: Describes what the cross is and its security meaning; no action.

title: Context: hash rate fact
body: Network hash rate reached 642 EH/s, up 1.8% on the week — a fresh all-time high for the security budget defending the network.

title: Context: MVRV education
body: MVRV compares market value to realised value — a way of seeing where price stands relative to the aggregate cost basis of all coins. It is one lens on holder behaviour among several; we use it as evidence, never as a signal to act.
rationale: Educational explanation framed as evidence, not a signal.
